title: VerifyReport
description: Envelope emitted by `aegis-boot verify --json`. Re-verifies every ISO on a stick against its sidecar checksum and reports a per-ISO verdict plus a summary tally. Used by CI / monitoring to audit that a stick's ISOs haven't bit-rotted, been replaced, or lost their sha256 sidecars.

isos.description: Per-ISO verdict. Always present (even as `[]` for an empty stick) so consumers see a stable field set.

mount_path.description: Filesystem mount path of the AEGIS_ISOS partition scanned.

tool_version.description: `aegis-boot` binary version that produced this envelope.

VerifyEntry.description:
One ISO's verdict inside a [`VerifyReport`]. The `verdict` field is the discriminator; variant-specific fields follow via `#[serde(flatten)]`. Consumer contract: branch on `verdict`, expect the fields documented for that variant.

Wire shape examples:

```text {"name": "ubuntu.iso", "verdict": "Verified", "digest": "…", "source": "sidecar"} {"name": "debian.iso", "verdict": "Mismatch", "actual": "…", "expected": "…", "source": "sidecar"} {"name": "alpine.iso", "verdict": "Unreadable", "source": "sidecar", "reason": "permission denied"} {"name": "fedora.iso", "verdict": "NotPresent"} ```

oneOf.description: sha256 of the ISO matches the sidecar's recorded digest.

source.description: Where the expected digest came from (e.g. `"sidecar"` for the on-partition `.sha256` file).

oneOf.description: sha256 of the ISO does NOT match the sidecar — either media corruption or a replaced/tampered file. Trust-chain breaking.

actual.description: Computed sha256 of the ISO on disk.

source.description: Where the expected digest came from.

oneOf.description: The ISO file exists but couldn't be opened / hashed.

source.description: Where the expected digest came from (so the operator knows which sidecar to reconcile against after restoring access to the file).

oneOf.description: An ISO referenced elsewhere (e.g. in the attestation manifest's `isos` list) is not on the partition.

VerifySummary.description: Tally of per-ISO verdicts in a [`VerifyReport`]. `any_failure` is the summary bit downstream tooling (CI, dashboards) branches on.

any_failure.description: True iff at least one of `mismatch`, `unreadable`, or `not_present` is non-zero. The overall stick-health bit.

mismatch.description: Count with `verdict: "Mismatch"` — sha256 differed from sidecar. A serious trust-chain signal; consumer should surface aggressively.

not_present.description: Count with `verdict: "NotPresent"` — referenced in an attestation manifest but missing from the partition.

total.description: Total ISOs scanned. Equals the length of [`VerifyReport::isos`].

unreadable.description: Count with `verdict: "Unreadable"` — file exists but couldn't be opened / read (permission, bad media).
